summary: Restructures /materia:init's intake into a staged eight-chapter interview (AskUserQuestion-driven, every non-essential question skippable) and grows the scaffold product brief it materializes: § Goals & success, a tagline slot, Brand & color slots, and the 'Not yet decided' honest-gap convention; and lands five stage-review angles (design-coherence, design-feasibility, design-fidelity, architecture-grounding, architecture-coverage) under two new Gate tokens (design-stage, architecture-stage), growing the canonical review-angle count from seven to twelve and wiring the pre-human-gate design-stage review (design.md plus the design's visual half and committed snapshot) and the pre-plan-tasks architecture-stage review into ship-spec (and, over bug-analysis.md, into fix-bug).

changes.summary: The scaffold product brief (docs/product.md) gains a § Goals & success section, a tagline slot, explicit Brand & color slots, and a 'Not yet decided' skip convention; /materia:init's intake becomes a staged eight-chapter interview that fills them.
changes.detectionNotes: docs/product.md is materialized once at init and thereafter user-owned prose the repo freely edits; the absence of the new sections/slots in an installed brief is indistinguishable from a deliberate local edit, so no doctor check can honestly flag it. The interview behavior itself lives in the plugin skill and updates with the plugin.
changes.manualMigration: Update the materialized docs to match the current scaffold if desired: in docs/product.md add § Goals & success (why it exists / success metrics / milestones / business model), the Tagline slot under § Name & positioning, and the Brand & color bullet under § Design feel & taste — filling each from your own answers, or with an explicit 'Not yet decided — <what was asked>.' line; then extend the brief-content enumerations in CLAUDE.md, docs/README.md, and docs/standards/docs.md with goals. The staged-interview behavior lives in the plugin skill and updates with the plugin — no repo action needed for that part.
changes.notes: Discoverability: surfaced to schema-current repos in /materia:doctor's informational availableAdoptions listing (status/exit unaffected; absent from /materia:migrate output, which lists only mechanical steps) — acknowledge via /materia:migrate --acknowledge once adopted or considered (see 0.3.0-acknowledged-changes-field). No artifactSchema bump — a content change to a materialized, user-owned doc; no canonical-location or installed-artifact-set move. Classified optional (operator decision at intake): nothing in this plugin version consumes the new brief sections yet, so an installed repo loses no capability by keeping its older brief — the design/propose-spec grounding reads sections that already existed. Also folded under this entry: survey→interview rewording of init comments in the scaffold's MATERIA.md, glossary.md, and surface-map.md (comments deleted at materialization time).

changes.summary: Lands five stage-review angles (design-coherence, design-feasibility, design-fidelity, architecture-grounding, architecture-coverage) in the review-angle library and MATERIA.md § Review angles registry under two new Gate tokens, design-stage and architecture-stage (canonical count seven → twelve); ship-spec spawns the design-stage angles after design.md is authored, before the human design gate's first arrival — design-fidelity also reviewing the design's visual half and committed snapshot through a capability-gated visibility ladder (direct canvas read, orchestrator-inlined read-back, or snapshot alone; skipped-and-recorded on a repo-side design with no visual half), with the first snapshot export pinned to precede the review — and the architecture-stage angles after architecture.md returns ok, before plan-tasks, while fix-bug spawns the architecture-stage angles analogously over bug-analysis.md — each point an orchestrator-spawned, ≤3-round adversarial loop (ship-spec/SKILL.md § Stage reviews (design & architecture)) over the registry's default per-angle tier, which the orchestrator may override per run.
changes.detectionNotes: The review-angle library (.materia/review-angles/) is a forkable, hand-reconciled artifact the user repo freely edits — doctor's inspect() never scans it, and a missing angle file or registry row is not mechanically distinguishable from a deliberate local removal. ship-spec/SKILL.md § Stage reviews (design & architecture)'s zero-rows path already handles this at runtime, not a doctor check: zero registered rows for a point skip that review point and record `stage-review(<point>): skipped (no <token> angles registered)` in § Notes — never an error. The MATERIA.md § Review angles and § Design tool prose, the review-angles README intro, and the status-template stage-review § Notes convention blocks are all hand-reconciled in files the user repo freely edits — no doctor check can honestly flag their absence.
changes.manualMigration: Update the materialized scaffold to match the current scaffold: the five new .materia/review-angles/{design-coherence,design-feasibility,design-fidelity,architecture-grounding,architecture-coverage}.md angle files (copy from the current scaffold — mission-only bodies); MATERIA.md § Review angles (the five new registry rows, the design-stage/architecture-stage Gate-token definitions, the canonical-count/self-description rewrite from seven to twelve, and the stage-review tier-default+override sentence); MATERIA.md § Design tool's Authoring budget bullet (the pre-gate stage-review touch pricing — up to 3 further authoring touches on top of the existing ≤4 gate-bound touches, each priced alike); .materia/review-angles/README.md's intro (now naming both review kinds — the post-implementation fan-out and the stage-review points); the stage-review § Notes convention blocks in docs/specs/_templates/status.md (both design-stage and architecture-stage) and docs/bugs/_templates/status.md (architecture-stage only — no design-stage point on the bug lane); the design template's Feedback-log comment (naming both revision channels — a design-gate revision round or a pre-gate stage-review revision round); and docs/specs/README.md + docs/bugs/README.md's pipeline-depiction updates (the stage-reviewed annotations on the design/architecture/bug-analysis rows and the review-fan-out row's split between its seven post-implementation angles and the five stage-review-only registry rows). The loops themselves — ship-spec § Stage reviews (design & architecture), fix-bug § Bug-analysis stage review, spawn-contract Block 3a, and the design/architecture/bug-analysis stage-skill reconciliation — live in the plugin skills and update with the plugin; a repo that skips this adoption simply has its stage reviews skip-and-record (the zero-rows path above), losing the capability but breaking nothing.
changes.notes: Discoverability: surfaced to schema-current repos in /materia:doctor's informational availableAdoptions listing (status/exit unaffected; absent from /materia:migrate output, which lists only mechanical steps) — acknowledge via /materia:migrate --acknowledge once adopted or considered (see 0.3.0-acknowledged-changes-field). No artifactSchema bump — angle-library membership and the hand-reconciled MATERIA.md/status-template prose sit outside the tracked installed-artifact contract (release/README.md). The `ledger` surface token is deliberately omitted: appending a Change entry is not a change to ledger structure/machinery (the 0.3.0 convention — sibling entries that merely append do not self-list `ledger`). Classified recommended (operator decision at intake): a repo that skips adoption loses the stage-review capability entirely — the loops skip-and-record at both points — while an untouched repo keeps working unaffected; a real capability loss, not a graceful degrade-to-default, the same posture as 0.3.0-design-conformance-angle.
